message:
feat(engine): adaptive S3-FIFO cache with value-aware admission (RGC-620B)

Implements the adaptive variant of S3-FIFO (bd-1lsy.7.20.2) that
dynamically adjusts the small-queue/main-queue split ratio based on
observed ghost-hit rates and gates admission on per-entry value scores:

Adaptive split policy:
- AdaptiveSplitConfig: configurable min/max small-queue fraction
  (millionths), max step per epoch, and epoch length
- AdaptiveSplitState tracks epoch accesses, ghost hits, misses, and
  adaptation count; at each epoch boundary the small queue grows if
  ghost-hit ratio exceeds a threshold, shrinks otherwise, bounded by
  max_step_per_epoch for deterministic replay stability

Value-aware admission:
- ValueAdmissionConfig: initial threshold (millionths), EMA alpha for
  threshold updates from evicted values, floor value below which entries
  are unconditionally rejected
- ValueAdmissionState: running threshold with denied/admitted counters
- AdmissionVerdict record for each insertion attempt

Core types:
- S3FifoAdaptiveConfig combining resident_capacity, initial_small_queue,
  ghost_queue, adaptive_split, and value_admission with full validation
- ValueAnnotatedTraceAccess/ValueAnnotatedTraceCase: value-tagged trace
  format for simulation
- S3FifoAdaptiveMetrics: extends baseline metrics with value_denied_count,
  value_admitted_count, admission_verdicts, final_threshold, and
  adaptation_count
- simulate_s3fifo_adaptive(): runs the adaptive simulation over a trace
- annotate_trace_with_default_values(), default_s3fifo_adaptive_config()

Integration tests (+494 lines):
- Config validation: zero capacity, small==capacity, zero ghost, min>=max
  fraction, max>1M, zero epoch, alpha>1M
- Empty trace, single insert+hit, value denial, threshold denial
- Ghost-hit adaptation grows small queue over multiple epochs
- Eviction updates admission threshold via EMA
- High-value entries survive while low-value are denied
- Scan-heavy workload: value floor filters scan traffic
- Full config round-trip serialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
